decision.reason: The GCP adapter has live evidence for row access policies, direct column data masking, policy-tag column access, BigQuery descriptions, core evidence writes and governance-ledger evidence write/readback for declared governance intent. It now exposes a non-mutating governance-reconcile command that reads native BigQuery row policies, data policies, policy tags, descriptions and ContractForge governance evidence, then compares them with declared contract intent. It has not validated tag-based masking, policy-tag-backed data masking, overwrite-retention behavior or automatic repair/delete flows.
decision.planner_behavior: Claim the validated BigQuery governance surfaces, deterministic governance ledger/reconciliation artifacts, smoke-runtime governance evidence write/readback and non-mutating reconciliation readback. Keep tag-based masking, policy-tag-backed masking, overwrite-retention governance and automatic repair/delete as REVIEW_REQUIRED.

required_before_promotion: Validate policy-tag-backed data masking if tag-based masking is promoted into stable scope.
Validate overwrite and replace-table behavior preserves or intentionally reapplies row policies, data policies and policy tags.
Validate automatic access-policy repair/delete behavior if ContractForge ever mutates unmanaged grants/policies.
Run a cross-adapter governance E2E project covering apply, readback, restricted-principal enforcement, evidence rows and failure evidence.
acceptance: Stable-final GCP claims may include the validated governance smokes, deterministic governance ledger/reconciliation artifacts, smoke-runtime governance evidence write/readback and non-mutating governance-reconcile readback. They must explicitly exclude tag-based masking, overwrite retention and automatic governance repair/delete until linked evidence exists.
